audit_method: direct URL fetch — strip-first repair, 2026-06-12. Each source URL fetched directly with curl or PDF text extraction; no WebSearch snippets used.

rows.supporting_quote: The market analysis provided by concluded that the proposed project will be the first LIHTC family property developed in Rifle in nearly 20 years and will be the only LIHTC family property with any vacancy upon construction completion and opening in the Primary Market Area (PMA). ... There has not been a family property LIHTC project built in Rifle since 2002. ... This 100% affordable development will serve individuals or families at 50%, 60%, 70%, and 80% AMI, and 14 units will be Project-Based Vouchers units serving individuals or families at or below 30% AMI.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. Supports first-family-LIHTC timing, unit mix/affordability, and 14 PBV units.

rows.supporting_quote: Evergreen executives joined Rifle Mayor Sean Strode, Colorado State Sen. Perry Will, other city officials and project partners for a ceremonial groundbreaking on Sept. 27. Filling a vacant 4.7-acre site at 115, 123 and 131 Park Avenue, Rifle Apartments is the first family-oriented Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) project built in Rifle over the past 20 years, aligning with the city’s Downtown Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) Strategic Plan and Comprehensive Plan.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Supports groundbreaking date; grand opening and lease-up are supported by s4.

rows.supporting_quote: Chicago-based Evergreen Real Estate Group and Rifle city officials celebrate the grand opening of Rifle Apartments, a new 60-unit affordable rental community, April 17 at 115, 123 and 131 Park Ave. in Rifle. ... The development is currently 95% leased, according to a news release from the company.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Supports grand opening date and 95% lease-up.

rows.supporting_quote: Rifle Housing Authority was established in 1979 and was designed to provide affordable rental apartments for senior and disabled residents. ... We have 60 apartments that offer HUD rental assistance/Section 8 and 46 apartments that are affordable housing.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Supports RHA establishment, mission, and unit categories.

rows.supporting_quote: Rifle Housing Authority, which started in 1979, currently has 106 low-income apartments for senior and disabled residents. 60 of the current apartments offer HUD rental assistance, and 46 are part of affordable housing. Currently, 150 people are waiting for an apartment to open up in the existing four-plexes, duplexes, and apartments.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Supports total units, HUD/affordable split, and roughly 150-household waitlist.

rows.supporting_quote: The City of Rifle has committed to explore local funding sources to support Rifle Apartments in the amount of $600,000 and will also be applying to the Department of Local Affairs for HB 22-1304 Infrastructure and Strong Communities Funds in the amount of $5,000,000. If awarded, this project has received a letter of support that qualifies it for the Division of Housing Gap Funds in the amount of $3,000,000, based on the rural location. ... Included in our application materials is a Letter of Support from the City of Rifle for the Rifle Apartments project, signed by all members of the City Council, including Mayor Ed Green.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. Supports City contribution, letter of support, HB 22-1304 request, and DOH gap-funds request.
